events.title: Southern California Ports Report Strong May Volumes
events.description: The twin ports of Southern California reported strong cargo volumes in May, rebounding from the previous year's decline caused by tariffs and other disruptions. Positive near-term outlooks suggest continued recovery in port activity.

events.title: CMA CGM introduces PSS for China to Southern/East Africa
events.description: CMA CGM will implement a Peak Season Surcharge (PSS) of US$450 per TEU from North and Central China to ports in Southern Africa, including Durban, starting 21 June 2026.

events.title: Singapore VLSFO Bunker Fuel Constraints
events.description: VLSFO bunker fuel availability in Singapore remains constrained, with suppliers recommending 10–14 day lead times for deliveries. HSFO and LSMGO availability is also limited, with lead times tightening to 7–10 days.

events.title: IMF warns of economic fallout despite Hormuz Strait reopening
events.description: The IMF remains on high alert regarding economic impacts from the Middle East conflict, despite a US-Iran agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Energy supplies are expected to take time to recover, signaling ongoing supply chain risks.

events.title: Hormuz Strait tanker traffic remains limited post-US-Iran deal
events.description: Oil tankers have begun exiting the Strait of Hormuz following a US-Iran agreement, but ship-tracking data indicates that traffic remains limited, suggesting ongoing uncertainty or restricted flow through the critical chokepoint.

events.title: Potential Hormuz Strait reopening after U.S.-Iran deal
events.description: A possible U.S.-Iran agreement is emerging, raising hopes for the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz after more than three months of disruption.

events.title: Russia Assembles LNG Shadow Fleet Ahead of EU Sanctions
events.description: The UK has sanctioned 27 tankers, including four LNG carriers recently purchased by Russia, as part of preparations for EU sanctions set to take effect in January 2027. This move signals a potential disruption in LNG shipping and trade flows.

events.title: High risk persists in Strait of Hormuz ahead of US-Iran deal
events.description: Shipping companies continue to face high risks transiting the Strait of Hormuz as a US-Iran peace deal is set for June 19. Security officials warn normal traffic may take weeks to resume even after the deal is signed.

events.title: US–Iran interim deal to ease Strait of Hormuz tensions
events.description: The US and Iran have reached an interim deal to remove the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz within 30 days of the agreement signed on 19 June, with further negotiations planned over the next 60 days. The Strait is a critical global shipping chokepoint, and its de-escalation could significantly impact regional freight flows.

events.title: Chief Engineer of Containership Dali Faces Criminal Charges
events.description: Criminal charges have been filed against the chief engineer of the containership Dali, following an incident that caused significant disruption in Baltimore.

events.title: Captain of sanctioned tanker held for trial
events.description: A UK court ordered the captain of the sanctioned tanker Smyrtos held for trial on charges of sanctions violations, highlighting legal and regulatory actions impacting maritime trade.

events.title: Yokkaichi Port container volumes drop 6.7% in April
events.description: The Port of Yokkaichi processed 12,623 TEUs in April, a 6.7% decline year-on-year. Exports fell 8% to 6,914 TEUs and imports dropped 5.1% to 5,709 TEUs, indicating a slowdown in port activity.

events.title: ZPMC deploys IGVs at Qinzhou terminal expansion
events.description: ZPMC has delivered five Intelligent Guided Vehicles (IGVs) for the expansion of the Qinzhou automated container terminal. The IGVs will support operations at Berths 9 and 10 with autonomous navigation and obstacle avoidance technology.

events.title: European hesitation on Hormuz naval deployment
events.description: European officials are hesitant to quickly commit naval forces to the Strait of Hormuz amid US pressure to secure the critical shipping route, raising concerns about potential delays in ensuring safe passage.

events.title: QatarEnergy to Restart LNG Production at Ras Laffan
events.description: QatarEnergy is prepared to quickly resume liquefied natural gas production at its Ras Laffan LNG plant and could reach full output within a month for facilities not impacted by Iranian strikes.

events.title: UK Sanctions Arctic LNG 2 Vessels
events.description: The UK has sanctioned four LNG carriers linked to Russia's Arctic LNG 2 project, targeting new vessels used to expand exports from the sanctioned development.

events.title: U.S. Lifts Iranian Oil Sanctions
events.description: The Trump administration announced it will immediately lift Iranian oil sanctions under a new agreement, allowing Iran to resume oil and fuel sales. This policy shift could significantly impact global oil trade flows and supply chain dynamics.

events.title: High-Risk Activity in Hormuz’s Southern Highway
events.description: A U.S. military document highlights ongoing high-risk activity along the 'Southern Highway' near the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint, amid tensions between Washington and Tehran.

events.title: U.S.-Iran deal expected to impact Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Oil prices fell as markets anticipate an interim U.S.-Iran peace agreement that could lead to the reopening of the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz, a key global oil shipping route.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz disruption impacts fertilizer markets
events.description: The ongoing disruption at the Strait of Hormuz is affecting fertilizer markets by limiting access to critical inputs like sulphur. This has shifted market dynamics from cost-based to access-based, with broader implications for food production.

events.title: France Prepares Hormuz Peacekeeping Mission
events.description: France, the UK, and allied nations are preparing to launch a peacekeeping mission in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint, to ensure safe passage following potential disruptions.

events.title: China-Russia rail freight rates surge due to container shortages
events.description: China-Russia container rail freight rates have doubled to $8,000 per FEU due to tight sea freight availability and container shortages. Increased demand for rail transport follows disrupted sea routes and slow return of empty containers.

events.title: Copenhagen Malmö Port adopts Portchain Connect for berth coordination
events.description: Copenhagen Malmö Port has joined the Portchain Connect Network to enhance berth planning and coordination with shipping lines through digital handshakes and secure data sharing.

events.title: Asia-U.S. West Coast ocean rate stability amid shipping uncertainty
events.description: Spot rates on the Asia-U.S. West Coast route remain unchanged at $4,836 per FEU as shippers rush to avoid upcoming fuel surcharges and price hikes from Asian manufacturers, signaling potential rate adjustments in the coming weeks.

events.title: Chinese tech exports to US surge after tariff removal
events.description: Chinese containerized exports to the US rose 35% year-on-year in May following the removal of Trump-era IEEPA tariffs, boosting shipments of solar and AI-related goods and increasing transpacific freight rates.

events.title: Challenge launches new cargo flights to India and China
events.description: Challenge Group is launching new freighter services from Liège to Mumbai and Shanghai, increasing cargo capacity and reflecting strong demand for Europe-Asia freight routes.

events.title: Bangladesh lifts foreign ownership cap on ICDs
events.description: Bangladesh has removed the foreign ownership cap on inland container depots (ICDs) and off-dock facilities, effective from 1 July, to attract more foreign direct investment into its logistics and port sectors.

events.title: Maersk implements new peak season surcharges
events.description: Maersk has announced a new Peak Season Surcharge (PSS) for shipments from Far East Asia to North Europe and the Mediterranean, effective from 7 July, and from South Korea from 16 July. This follows a broader trend of carriers increasing surcharges, adding to ongoing challenges for shippers.

events.title: Zanzibar Develops $560m Free Port at Mangapwani
events.description: Zanzibar has announced plans for a $560m free port and logistics zone at Mangapwani to boost regional trade and attract international shipping.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Reopening Dispute
events.description: The US claims the Strait of Hormuz will reopen by Friday, conflicting with allies' assessments. The strait is a critical route for global oil and LNG shipments.

events.title: US uses ship-to-ship oil transfers in Gulf
events.description: The U.S. military is conducting covert ship-to-ship oil transfers in the Gulf to maintain energy exports, using drones and helicopters to guide convoys to awaiting tankers.

events.title: Hormuz Strait Reopening and New Shipping Charges
events.description: A potential deal between the US and Iran has raised questions about Iran's plan to charge shipping fees through the Strait of Hormuz, prompting shipowners to reconsider financial implications and routing alternatives.

events.title: Maersk Maintains Gulf Restrictions Amid Hormuz Reopening
events.description: Maersk continues to enforce cargo restrictions and emergency surcharges in the Persian Gulf, indicating ongoing supply chain disruptions despite recent efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: ONE streamlines Adriatic Service for improved schedule integrity
events.description: Ocean Network Express (ONE) is restructuring its Adriatic Service 1 (AD1) to improve schedule reliability and reduce transit times. The service will remove calls at Aliaga and Piraeus, streamlining the route for a more efficient weekly operation.

events.title: Bulk-to-container ship conversion completed in China
events.description: The 80,000-dwt Kuang Chi Delta, the world's largest bulk carrier-to-container ship conversion, was delivered after a six-month project at Zhoushan Xinya Shipbuilding & Repair. This marks a milestone in the ship conversion sector.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz threat level remains severe
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz remains a high-risk area with reported explosions and ongoing military protection needs, despite recent diplomatic progress. Normal trade resumption is still uncertain.

events.title: CMA CGM imposes PSS on China-West Africa shipments
events.description: CMA CGM has announced a new Peak Season Surcharge (PSS) of US$750 per TEU for shipments from China to West Africa, effective 15 June 2026, impacting dry and reefer cargo under short-term contracts.

events.title: UK Charges Indian Captain of Russian Shadow Fleet Tanker
events.description: The UK has charged an Indian captain of a Russian shadow fleet tanker with violating sanctions on Russian oil exports. The incident is part of a broader operation targeting illicit Russian oil trade.

events.title: Ships sailing through Strait of Hormuz
events.description: U.S. President Donald Trump stated that ships loaded with oil are sailing through the Strait of Hormuz via the Southern 'Highway,' indicating ongoing activity in a critical global energy chokepoint.

events.title: US-Iran agreement eases Strait of Hormuz tensions
events.description: A peace agreement between the US and Iran signals progress toward restoring safety and stability in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global maritime chokepoint.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic near zero due to operational pressure
events.description: Between June 11 and June 14, commercial vessel traffic through the Strait of Hormuz dropped to nearly zero, with only one AIS-detected crossing. Increased IRGC small-craft activity and ongoing covert exports were reported amid sustained U.S. enforcement efforts.

events.title: U.S.-Iran Strait of Hormuz MOU Signed
events.description: A U.S.-Iranian memorandum of understanding on a 60-day ceasefire and reopening of the Strait of Hormuz has been signed, potentially easing tensions affecting this critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Cargo ship damages subsea cables in Finland
events.description: Finnish prosecutors have charged the captain and bosun of a small cargo ship for dragging its anchor and damaging two subsea telecom cables in December.

events.title: Philippine Passenger Vessel Grounds Off Palawan
events.description: A Philippine passenger vessel has run aground off the coast of Palawan, prompting a response from the Philippine Coast Guard. The incident occurred on Sunday and may affect local maritime operations.

events.title: EU Sanctions 24 Shipping Companies
events.description: The European Council imposed sanctions on 24 shipping companies, including Russian firms Gazprom and Lukoil, as part of broader measures targeting energy revenues and the military-industrial complex.

events.title: India Struggles to Auction Derelict Cargo Ship
events.description: India has been unable to auction a derelict cargo ship for nearly three years, causing ongoing logistical and legal challenges. The ship remains a concern for maritime authorities and the crew's union.

events.title: US–Iran Memorandum of Understanding impacts chokepoint crisis
events.description: The US–Iran Memorandum of Understanding signed on June 14 ends one phase of the crisis but does not resolve ongoing conditions affecting the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Bilbao Terminal installs largest quay crane in Atlantic Arc
events.description: CSP Iberian Bilbao Terminal has added a new super post-Panamax ship-to-shore crane, the largest in the Atlantic Arc, to enhance capacity for larger container vessels and improve efficiency at the Port of Bilbao.

events.title: Ships using southern transit corridor through Hormuz
events.description: Ships are reportedly using a southern transit corridor through the Strait of Hormuz, established during recent tensions with Iran, as an alternative route for maritime traffic.

events.title: Mine clearance delays in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Clearing mines from the Strait of Hormuz could take weeks, delaying the return to normal shipping traffic after a deal to reopen the waterway.

events.title: British intercepts Russian shadow fleet tanker
events.description: Royal Marine Commandos boarded a Russian Aframax tanker in the English Channel, likely linked to Russia's shadow fleet circumventing sanctions. The event highlights ongoing geopolitical tensions impacting maritime logistics and trade flows.

events.title: BHP Workers Strike Threatens Port Hedland Iron Ore Shipments
events.description: An impending strike by BHP workers at Australia's Port Hedland, a key iron ore hub, has raised concerns over potential shipment disruptions. Labor unions reported that a majority of members voted in favor of industrial action.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz reopening potential
events.description: Ongoing US-Iran ceasefire talks raise hopes for the Strait of Hormuz to reopen, which is vital for global oil exports. A sustainable agreement could quickly restore critical freight flows through this key maritime chokepoint.

events.title: Hormuz Strait traffic expected to rise with U.S.-Iran security deal
events.description: A leading oil tanker company CEO expressed optimism that ship traffic through the Strait of Hormuz will quickly increase if the U.S. and Iran reach a stable agreement to improve security in the strategic sea lane.

events.title: Asia-US container rates hit highest since July 2025
events.description: Container shipping rates from Asia to the US have risen to their highest levels since July 2025, driven by ongoing disruptions in the Middle East. Rates now range between $4,000-4,850 per 40-foot equivalent unit (FEU) to the US West Coast.

events.title: US & Iran peace agreement eases Strait of Hormuz tensions
events.description: The International Chamber of Shipping welcomed a US-Iran peace agreement, which could ease tensions affecting the Strait of Hormuz. Around 500 ships are currently waiting to pass through the critical chokepoint.

events.title: Dredging at Kwai Tsing Container Basin
events.location: Kwai Tsing Container Basin, Hong Kong
events.description: Dredging operations are underway around the clock in Kwai Tsing Container Basin, Hong Kong, and are expected to last approximately eight months.

events.title: Ships Engage in Firefight with Pirates Near Somalia
events.description: Commercial ships in the Gulf of Aden have reported coming under attack by pirates, with one incident involving a 30-minute firefight. The attacks are increasing in frequency and boldness, raising concerns for maritime security in the region.

events.title: ONE launches Riga-Portugal shipping service
events.description: Ocean Network Express has launched the Iberia Baltic Express, a weekly container service connecting the Port of Riga to Portuguese ports for the first time, enhancing freight access for Latvian trade.

events.title: Hormuz Strait diplomatic developments
events.description: A diplomatic framework has been announced between the US and Iran, but a formal transit regime for the Strait of Hormuz has not yet been restored, leaving uncertainty for maritime professionals routing cargo through the region.

events.title: U.S. tax proposal on Chinese ships impacts ag producers
events.description: A proposed tax on Chinese ships docking at U.S. ports could increase costs for American agricultural shippers, potentially putting some producers out of business.

events.title: U.S.-Iran peace deal eases Strait of Hormuz tensions
events.description: A peace agreement between the U.S. and Iran aims to end hostilities in the Arabian Gulf, potentially allowing shipping through the Strait of Hormuz to normalize after months of disruption.

events.title: Truckload rate contracts being voided
events.description: Shippers are being forced to reprice their entire book as contractual truckload rates set just a couple of months ago are no longer being honored, signaling a breakdown in routing guide stability.

events.title: MSC shifts transhipment hub from Colombo to Hambantota
events.description: MSC is transitioning transhipment operations from Colombo to Hambantota Port on its South Asia services, including the Far East-South Africa Ingwe service, as part of a strategic hub realignment.

events.title: Air cargo shippers delay contracts as rates may peak
events.description: Air cargo shippers are delaying tender decisions amid concerns that historically high freight rates may have peaked, influenced by the Middle East crisis. Market data shows global air cargo spot rates averaged $3.40 per kg in May, up 41% year-on-year.

events.title: Persian Gulf conflict resolution impacts Sub-Saharan Africa trade
events.description: The ongoing conflict in the Persian Gulf has severely disrupted container trade volumes to Sub-Saharan Africa, but a tentative agreement between the involved parties offers hope for a recovery in trade later in the year.

events.title: Container cartel collusion during pandemic
events.description: Class-action lawsuits allege major container manufacturers colluded to restrict output and inflate prices during the pandemic, causing significant supply chain disruptions.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Reopening Delayed Amid Iran Deal
events.description: The global shipping industry welcomed a U.S.-Iran agreement to end conflict near the Strait of Hormuz, but maritime groups warned that full reopening of the critical waterway will take time, causing ongoing uncertainty for freight flows.

events.title: Armed skiff attacks on ships off Yemen
events.description: Two merchant vessels were attacked by armed skiffs off Yemen in the Gulf of Aden, raising concerns about maritime security and potential disruptions to shipping routes.

events.title: Hormuz Strait Trade Recovery Delayed
events.description: Analysts warn that the restoration of oil and gas flows through the Strait of Hormuz following a US-Iran interim agreement may take months, despite positive reactions in oil markets.

events.title: EU Sanctions Russian Oil Shipping Subsidiaries
events.description: The European Union has imposed sanctions on the shipping arms of Gazprom and Lukoil, targeting their role in transporting Russian oil via high-risk routes and the shadow fleet. This move disrupts Russian oil logistics and escalates trade policy pressures on Moscow.

events.title: Shipowners cautious on Hormuz Strait reopening
events.description: Shipowners and traders are seeking more details before resuming transits through the Strait of Hormuz following a US-Iran deal aimed at reopening the critical waterway. Concerns remain over the safety and feasibility of resuming operations after previous failed attempts.

events.title: US and Iran agree to reopen Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The United States and Iran have agreed on a framework deal to end their conflict and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint. The agreement is expected to restore normal freight flows through the region and ease market concerns.

events.title: UK Seizes Russian Shadow Fleet Tanker in English Channel
events.description: British forces seized the sanctioned Russian shadow fleet tanker Smyrtos in the English Channel, arresting a seafarer suspected of sanctions violations. The vessel was moved to an anchorage off England's south coast.

events.title: Panama flag decline due to US-China tensions
events.description: The Panama Ship Registry is experiencing a decline as tensions between the US and China impact global shipping. Shipowners are abandoning the Panama flag, with a 3.3% reduction in registered vessels so far this year.

events.title: Hormuz ceasefire deal sparks cautious optimism
events.description: A tentative US-Iran ceasefire deal has raised hopes for the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz after 107 days of disruption. However, experts caution that a return to normal shipping operations will be gradual and uncertain.

events.title: Shipowners Await Clarity on Hormuz Strait Deal
events.description: Shipowners and traders are seeking more details on a US-Iran deal aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormuz, as uncertainty persists over safe passage following months of disruptions.

events.title: U.S.-Iran Deal to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
events.description: U.S. and Iranian officials announced a preliminary agreement to end hostilities and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint. The deal could significantly impact oil shipments and regional freight flows.

events.title: PortMiami interim director to retire
events.description: The newly appointed interim director of PortMiami has announced his retirement at the end of the month, adding to ongoing turmoil at the port, including issues at a fuel depot.

events.title: Tanker hit in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A Hong Kong-flagged tanker was hit and damaged in the Strait of Hormuz by Iranian forces, according to maritime security sources.

events.title: US escorts oil tankers through Strait of Hormuz
events.description: US forces are conducting nighttime escort operations for oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz, facilitating the movement of over 20 ships on some nights following the removal of sea mines.

events.title: U.S. defends Hormuz actions after Indian sailors killed
events.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio defended American military actions in the Strait of Hormuz following Indian protests over the deaths of three Indian sailors. The incident occurred amid heightened tensions in the strategic waterway.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Closure Causes Vessel Idling
events.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz to most commercial shipping since late February 2026 has left approximately 1,550 vessels at anchor in the Arabian Gulf, Gulf of Oman, and adjacent areas, impacting around 20,000 seafarers and disrupting global freight flows.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Traffic Declines Amid Military Pressure
events.description: Commercial traffic through the Strait of Hormuz is significantly reduced despite the strait remaining technically open. Military activity and dark vessel operations are increasing, creating heightened uncertainty and operational constraints for maritime freight.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Blockade Impacts Tanker Orders
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz has been largely blocked since late February 2026, leading to increased tanker ordering activity for large carriers despite an ongoing energy crisis.

events.title: Israeli Strike Undermines Hormuz Ceasefire Deal
events.description: An Israeli strike hours after a reported ceasefire deal with Iran has cast doubt on the agreement, potentially escalating tensions in the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz, a key global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Indian Crew Rescued from Life Raft in Indian Ocean
events.description: U.S. Navy and a good Samaritan vessel rescued 14 Indian crewmembers from a life raft in the Indian Ocean after they became stranded.

events.title: UK Seizes Shadow Fleet Tanker in English Channel
events.description: British Royal Marines boarded the Cameroon-flagged Aframax Smyrtos in the English Channel on June 14, marking the UK's first seizure of a shadow fleet tanker.

events.title: Port of Long Beach cargo volumes surge 31.7% in May
events.description: The Port of Long Beach processed 842,030 TEUs in May 2026, a 31.7% increase from May 2025. Imports rose 40%, exports increased 32.9%, and empty container volumes climbed 21.8%.

events.title: Shimizu Port container volumes drop 6% in May
events.description: The Port of Shimizu processed 33,808 TEUs of foreign-trade containers in May, a 6% decrease from the same period last year, with notable declines in exports and loaded containers.

events.title: British intercept Russian shadow fleet tanker
events.description: British armed forces intercepted a sanctioned Russian shadow fleet oil tanker in the English Channel, marking the first time they have led an operation to disrupt Russian oil exports.

events.title: U.S. defends Hormuz enforcement after India protests
events.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio defended Washington's enforcement operations in the Strait of Hormuz following an Indian protest over an attack that killed three Indian mariners.

events.title: UK Boards Sanctioned Russian Shadow Fleet Tanker
events.description: The UK has boarded a sanctioned Russian shadow fleet tanker in a direct interdiction operation, marking a significant escalation in efforts to disrupt illicit oil shipments linked to Russia.

events.title: Rescue of 14 Indian mariners near Hormuz shipping route
events.description: A merchant vessel and U.S. Navy forces rescued 14 Indian mariners after their ship became disabled and was abandoned near the Hormuz shipping route, a critical global chokepoint.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz authority split impacts shipping
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz no longer has a single governing authority, creating a complex and contradictory operating environment for commercial shipping.

events.title: Ocean Alliance adds Gdansk to Europe-Far East service
events.description: Ocean Alliance has extended its Europe–Far East AEU7 service to include a new call at Gdansk, operated by COSCO Shipping Lines and OOCL, starting in August.

events.title: Pilbara Ports throughput drops 1% in May 2026
events.description: Pilbara Ports reported a 1% decrease in monthly throughput in May 2026, with Port Hedland's iron ore exports down 3% year-on-year to 51 million tonnes.

events.title: Trump Announces Deal to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
events.description: President Donald Trump announced an interim deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end the conflict with Iran, which could significantly impact global shipping and supply chain operations.

events.title: Ukrainian drone attack damages Russian port
events.description: A Ukrainian drone attack caused a fire at a sea terminal in Temryuk port, Russia, killing one person and disrupting port operations.

events.title: Tanker Struck by Projectile in Arabian Sea
events.description: A tanker reported being struck by a projectile, raising concerns about maritime security. Indian officials denied the vessel was theirs, while Pakistan was reportedly working on a statement.

events.title: Tanker attacked near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A commercial tanker was struck by an unidentified projectile near the Strait of Hormuz, an area linked to a covert U.S.-coordinated shipping corridor. The incident raises concerns about security and potential disruptions to global shipping in this critical chokepoint.

events.title: China Builds New Structures at Scarborough Shoal
events.description: China has constructed new structures at Scarborough Shoal within the Philippine exclusive economic zone, raising concerns over territorial disputes and potential impacts on maritime navigation and regional stability.

events.title: Shadow Fleet Tanker Master Pleads Guilty to Disobeying USCG
events.description: The master of the shadow fleet tanker Bella 1 has pleaded guilty to ignoring U.S. Coast Guard orders during a weeks-long pursuit. The incident highlights ongoing enforcement actions against illicit oil tankers operating outside regulatory compliance.

events.title: Maersk launches new Asia-South America shipping service
events.location: Far East to West Coast South America
events.description: Maersk is launching the AC1 service to connect the Far East with the West Coast of South America, enhancing freight connectivity between Asia and Latin American markets.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz shipping attacks
events.description: Global shipping industry associations condemn 46 verified attacks on commercial shipping in and around the Strait of Hormuz since 28 February 2026, with 14 incidents reported recently. The attacks pose a significant threat to seafarers and global freight flows.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Blockage Disrupts Energy Flows
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz has been largely blocked since late February, causing significant disruption to global energy shipments and contributing to an unprecedented energy crisis.

events.title: Middle East aluminum production disrupted by conflict
events.description: Supply chain shocks and Iranian strikes at major smelters in the UAE and Bahrain are forecast to reduce 2026 Middle Eastern aluminum production by up to 2 million metric tons, causing a shift toward recycled aluminum as a necessity.

events.title: Dangote refinery cuts West Africa clean product imports
events.description: The ramp-up of Nigeria's 650,000 b/d Dangote refinery has reduced clean product imports into West Africa by 23%, altering tanker routes and regional freight flows.

events.title: CMA CGM adjusts Northern Europe and Mediterranean services
events.description: CMA CGM has merged its Dunkirk Express service into the Helsingborg Express, creating an expanded Northern Europe loop that now includes Hamburg and increases coverage in the Eastern Mediterranean.

events.title: Trump raises doubts on USMCA renewal
events.description: Mexico remained the top US trade partner in April, but uncertainty grows over the future of the USMCA trade agreement as Donald Trump raises doubts about its renewal, potentially disrupting North American supply chains.

events.title: U.S. and Iran Near Deal to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The U.S. and Iran are close to finalizing a deal to end their war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint that has been disrupted for months.

events.title: Faulty Switch Causes Engine Shutdown on Iron Ore Carrier
events.description: A fully laden iron ore carrier nearly grounded in a busy Australian shipping channel in 2025 after a faulty switch caused an erroneous engine shutdown. The incident highlights operational risks and potential disruptions in maritime freight flows.

events.title: Gulftainer develops Al Dhaid multi-modal trade corridor
events.description: Gulftainer has announced plans to develop the Al Dhaid Multi-Modal Trade Corridor, a 150-hectare logistics hub with an annual capacity of 1.5 million TEUs, positioned as a capacity extension for Khorfakkan Port on the UAE's East Coast.

events.title: CU Lines deploys vessels on Hercules service
events.description: CU Lines will begin deploying its own vessels on the Hercules service between Southeast Asia and India/Pakistan from July, increasing its role from slot buyer to active operator in the joint service with X-Press Feeders and T.S. Lines.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Closure Impact Reassessed
events.description: Traders and shippers report that the disruption of Gulf oil exports due to the Iran war and the declared closure of the Strait of Hormuz is less severe than initially feared, affecting crude supply and oil price forecasts.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Reopening Near as US-Iran Peace Deal Looms
events.description: Shipowners are closely monitoring a potential peace deal between the US and Iran, which could lead to the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. Tanker owners are divided, with some predicting a surge in activity if the waterway reopens.

events.title: Hong Kong cable laying operations
events.location: Tseung Kwan O to Hong Kong Eastern Boundary
events.description: Cable laying operations are scheduled to take place from Tseung Kwan O to the Eastern Boundary of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region for approximately seven months starting 12 Jun 2026, potentially impacting port activities.

events.title: U.S.-Iran deal proposed to unblock Hormuz Strait
events.description: A proposed U.S.-Iran Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) would see Iran commit to reopening the Strait of Hormuz and the U.S. lift oil sanctions. The deal, if finalized, could significantly impact global oil shipments through this critical chokepoint.

events.title: Tensions Over Hormuz Strait Transits
events.description: Iran has claimed to have stopped transits through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint, while the U.S. has rebuffed these claims, heightening regional tensions and raising concerns over potential disruptions to oil and freight flows.

events.title: Collision of Two Harbor Craft in Singapore
events.description: Three people were killed when two harbor workboats collided near Pasir Panjang in Singapore on June 12. The incident may impact local port operations and harbor traffic.

events.title: Bangkok Port opens new cargo facility
events.description: The Port Authority of Thailand has opened a new 22,000-square-meter cargo-handling facility at Bangkok Port to improve efficiency, reduce congestion, and streamline barge operations and customs procedures.

events.title: ANL implements rate increase for Fiji cargo
events.description: ANL has announced a General Rate Increase (GRI) for cargo shipments to Fiji from multiple regions, effective July 1, 2026. The rate increase applies to both dry and refrigerated cargo.

events.title: Attacks on Seafarers in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The shipping industry has condemned attacks on commercial vessels in and around the Strait of Hormuz, where the death toll has reached 14. The situation poses a significant threat to global freight flows through this critical chokepoint.

events.title: US Ships Escort Oil Tankers Through Hormuz
events.description: U.S. forces are escorting oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz at night, moving over 20 ships per night, amid heightened security concerns in the region.

events.title: US Strike Kills Indian Seafarers, Sparks Outcry
events.description: A U.S. military strike killed three Indian seafarers, prompting grief and calls for action in Deoria, India. The incident has raised concerns about the safety of Indian maritime workers in conflict zones.

events.title: U.S.-Iran peace deal hopes ease Strait of Hormuz tensions
events.description: Oil prices fell as reports of a potential U.S.-Iran peace deal raised hopes for the Strait of Hormuz reopening, easing concerns over disruptions to global oil shipments through this critical chokepoint.

events.title: Adnoc LNG tanker transits Strait of Hormuz
events.description: An Adnoc-linked LNG tanker, Umm Al Ashtan, has transited the Strait of Hormuz and is en route to an LNG terminal in India. The vessel re-emerged after going dark and is expected to arrive on May 31.

events.title: Somali piracy structures are strengthening
events.description: An uptick in Somali piracy has been reported in 2026, with seven hijacks recorded involving various vessels. As of early June, three ships and their crews are being held for ransom off the Somali coast.

events.title: HaminaKotka cargo volumes drop 11.5% in 2026
events.description: The Port of HaminaKotka handled 5.29 million tonnes of cargo in the first five months of 2026, an 11.5% decline from the same period in 2025. Reduced volumes of dry bulk, sawn goods, wood pulp, paper, and general cargo contributed to the drop.

events.title: CMA CGM implements $1000/TEU PSS for Asia-Europe/Mediterranean shipments
events.description: CMA CGM has announced a new Peak Season Surcharge (PSS) of $1,000 per TEU for shipments from Asia to North Europe, the Mediterranean, and North Africa, effective 1 July 2026. The surcharge reflects increased operational costs and is expected to impact freight rates and trade flows.

events.title: MSC shifts Ingwe hub to Hambantota
events.description: MSC is replacing Colombo with Hambantota as the northbound Sri Lankan hub call on its Ingwe service, adding an extra vessel to increase capacity.

events.title: Rotterdam Food Hub Expansion Agreed
events.description: The Port of Rotterdam, Necron Group, and PTP Group have signed an agreement to develop 38 hectares of logistics infrastructure for chilled and frozen agrifood products in Europoort, including new quay walls, cold storage, and terminal facilities.

events.title: Valencia Port reconfigures exit lanes to improve traffic flow
events.description: Valenciaport has reconfigured the southern exit gate lanes at the Port of Valencia to improve traffic flow by separating vehicle types, including light vehicles, flatbed trucks, empty container trucks, and loaded cargo trucks.

events.title: Faster Labor Contracts Act Passes House
events.description: The Faster Labor Contracts Act (H.R. 5408) passed the House, requiring employers to reach a first union contract within 120 days or face government arbitration. This could impact labor relations in the freight and trucking sectors.

events.title: Port of Los Angeles forecasts 7% container volume decline
events.description: The Port of Los Angeles forecasts a 7% decline in container volume, projecting 9.3 million twenty-foot equivalent units. This follows the approval of a $3.4 billion annual budget for fiscal 2026-2027.

events.title: EU auto sector disrupted by US tariffs and Chinese imports
events.description: European automakers are facing a significant supply chain disruption due to US tariff hikes and rising Chinese vehicle imports, impacting freight flows and logistics operations.

events.title: High air freight rates persist despite capacity recovery
events.description: Air freight rates remain elevated despite increased capacity and lower fuel costs, driven by ongoing demand from the AI sector and lingering effects of the Iran conflict.

events.title: Asia-Europe freight rates rise due to Red Sea disruptions
events.description: Container spot freight rates on the transpacific and Asia-Europe trades have risen for six consecutive weeks, driven by ongoing disruptions from the Red Sea crisis and the effective closure of the Suez Canal for most Asia-Europe and some Asia-North America routes.

events.title: US-Iran interim deal to reopen Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The US and Iran are nearing an interim agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint, potentially easing tensions that have disrupted maritime freight flows.

events.title: EU Increasing Russian LNG Imports Ahead of Ban
events.description: Despite efforts to phase out Russian liquefied natural gas, the EU is importing more of it now than at the same time last year, indicating a policy and supply chain adjustment challenge.

events.title: CMA CGM implements new FAK rates on Asia-Europe and Europe-ISC routes
events.description: CMA CGM has announced new Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates for key trade lanes between Asia, Europe, the Mediterranean, North Africa, India, and Pakistan. The updated rates will take effect from 1 July 2026, signaling a potential shift in freight pricing strategies.

events.title: WTIV incident damages wind blades at Esbjerg Port
events.description: On June 10, the Brave Tern WTIV collided with a crane at the Port of Esbjerg, damaging offshore wind turbine blades and causing the vessel to be detained due to high winds and poor weather.

events.title: Fortescue bulk carrier loses propulsion near Port Hedland
events.description: A Fortescue bulk carrier, the FMG Nicola, lost propulsion while departing Port Hedland, prompting fleetwide engine management revisions. The incident was investigated by the Australian Transport Safety Bureau.

events.title: Hormuz shipping crisis escalates with tanker strikes
events.description: The Hormuz shipping crisis intensified this week with attacks between the US and Iran and tanker strikes off Oman, resulting in the deaths of three seafarers. Hopes for a resolution rose after President Trump suggested a potential US-Iran peace deal could be signed soon.

events.title: Port Hedland Iron Ore Exports Decline in May 2026
events.description: The Port of Port Hedland reported a 3% decrease in iron ore exports in May 2026, with throughput dropping to 51Mt compared to the same period in 2025. This decline may indicate operational or logistical challenges affecting the port's export capacity.

events.title: Containers fall from boxship onto bunker vessel in Antwerp
events.description: Four containers fell from a boxship onto a bunker vessel in the Port of Antwerp, raising concerns about port safety and potential disruptions to operations.

events.title: Explosive traces found on cruise ship in Kiel
events.description: A bomb squad was called to the port of Kiel after traces of explosives were found on a consignment during a boarding of the cruise ship Mein Schiff Relax.

events.title: Missile removed from Greek-owned VLCC Olympic Life
events.description: The Indian Navy's EOD specialists have removed an unexploded missile from inside the hull of the Greek-owned VLCC Olympic Life. The incident poses a significant safety risk and could impact operations in the region.

events.title: U.S. Military Aids Ships Through Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A U.S. military operation, reportedly confirmed by an industry advisory, has assisted commercial ships transiting the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz, indicating potential ongoing disruptions or heightened security concerns in the region.

events.title: Increased Red Sea Threat from Houthi Activity
events.description: The Red Sea threat has intensified following the Houthis' recent announcement, raising concerns for maritime security and potential disruptions to shipping routes.

events.title: LPG flows through Hormuz amid tightened blockade
events.description: Limited LPG cargo shipments are reaching South Asia through deceptive shipping practices as the Strait of Hormuz faces increased enforcement. Iranian crude exports remain heavily constrained, while U.S. enforcement expands and commercial traffic adjusts to new risks.

events.title: WTIV loses control in Danish port, damages wind turbine blades
events.description: A vessel lost control in the Port of Esbjerg, Denmark, damaging blades intended for an offshore wind farm. High winds and poor weather are cited as likely causes.

events.title: Tanker attacked near Strait of Hormuz
events.location: Strait of Hormuz, off coast of Oman
events.description: The tanker MT SETTEBELLO was attacked near the Strait of Hormuz, causing a fire on board and leaving three seafarers missing. The incident has been condemned by the International Maritime Organization.

events.title: Rail merger raises ag freight rate and service concerns
events.description: Agricultural retailers warn that the proposed Union Pacific-Norfolk Southern rail merger could lead to higher freight rates and reduced service quality, disrupting supply chain operations.

events.title: U.S. Navy strike on tanker causes fatalities
events.description: The manager of the tanker Settebello has accused the U.S. Navy of causing the deaths of three Indian seafarers during a strike, challenging the military's justification for the attack. The incident raises concerns about maritime safety and potential disruptions to shipping in the region.

events.title: BHP Workers Vote to Strike at Port Hedland Iron Ore Hub
events.description: Hundreds of BHP workers at Port Hedland in Western Australia have voted to strike, raising the risk of disruptions to iron ore shipments from a major global export hub.

events.title: Trump administration offsets Hormuz tolls using Iranian funds
events.description: The Trump administration has announced plans to reimburse commercial vessels for tolls paid to Iran's self-declared Strait of Hormuz transit authority using seized Iranian assets, potentially altering freight compliance and routing decisions.

events.title: Tolo Harbour Oil Terminal Repairs
events.description: Repair works at the oil terminal in Tolo Harbour, Hong Kong, have begun and are expected to last approximately eight months, potentially affecting port operations and freight flows.

events.title: LNG shipment arrives at Thi Vai LNG Port
events.description: On June 7th, the METHANE MICKIE HARPER vessel carrying 73,000 tons of LNG from Canada arrived at Thi Vai LNG Port in Vietnam. This is part of increased LNG and LPG imports by PV GAS to meet domestic energy demand.

events.title: US military strikes oil tanker in Gulf of Oman
events.description: A US military strike on the Palau-flagged oil tanker MT Settebello in the Gulf of Oman resulted in the deaths of three Indian sailors. The attack occurred after the US accused the vessel of not complying with military directives.

events.title: Korean LNG Carrier Passes Hormuz Blockade
events.description: A Korean-flagged LNG carrier successfully navigated the Strait of Hormuz after being stranded due to an ongoing blockade, following the earlier escape of a tanker. The event highlights ongoing tensions and disruptions in this critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: COSCO imposes emergency bunker surcharge on US cargo
events.description: COSCO Shipping Lines has introduced an Emergency Bunker Surcharge (EBS) on cargo moving to or via US East Coast, Gulf Coast, and West Coast ports through Trans-Atlantic and Trans-Pacific services, affecting freight costs and trade flows.

events.title: Middle East tensions ease, impacting bunker market
events.description: Easing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East have led to a decline in the global bunker market as concerns over supply disruptions have lessened. The 380 HSFO index dropped below $700/MT during Week 24.

events.title: Conservative group targets U.S. Jones Act for repeal
events.description: A conservative political action network, supported by Charles Koch, is pushing to repeal the Jones Act, which currently requires ships carrying cargo between U.S. ports to be U.S.-owned, -built, and -crewed.

events.title: Explosive hauler incident on I-75 near Chattanooga
events.description: A trailer carrying fireworks caught fire and exploded for 25 minutes on June 6 on I-75 outside Chattanooga. The driver lacked hazmat endorsement, placards, or shipping papers, raising safety and compliance concerns.

events.title: Panama Canal vessel draft restrictions due to El Niño
events.description: The Panama Canal Authority announced its first vessel draft restriction of 2026 due to El Niño conditions, which NOAA has confirmed are developing into one of the strongest on record. This could impact global shipping as the canal is a critical transit point for international trade.

events.title: U.S. Reaffirms Hormuz Strait Open Amid Iranian Claims
events.description: U.S. Central Command reaffirmed the Strait of Hormuz is open to commercial shipping after an Iran-linked group claimed authority over the waterway. The statement comes amid heightened tensions affecting a critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: US-Iran escalation threatens Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Oil prices have risen due to renewed US strikes in Iran and threats of further action. Iran has warned it may close the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global oil chokepoint, in response.

events.title: APAC coal demand surges due to Middle East conflict
events.description: The Middle East conflict has damaged Gulf energy infrastructure, prompting a 150 million tonne surge in thermal coal demand in the Asia-Pacific region as energy security becomes a priority.

events.title: Attack on tanker MT Settebello near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The International Maritime Organization condemned the attack on the tanker MT Settebello near the Strait of Hormuz, with three seafarers reported missing. The incident raises concerns over regional security and potential disruptions to maritime trade in a key global chokepoint.

events.title: ARA Hub Bunker Fuel Availability Tightens
events.description: Bunker fuel availability remains tight in the ARA hub, with recommended lead times for VLSFO and HSFO supplies now at 7-8 days. Independently held fuel oil stocks have remained flat in June.

events.title: Hormuz Strait normalisation delayed
events.description: Hapag-Lloyd CEO stated that normalisation of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz will take at least three months after access is restored, indicating ongoing disruption to global freight flows.

events.title: Sanctioned Russian tankers bypassing English Channel
events.description: Western-sanctioned Russian oil tankers are rerouting to avoid the English Channel, with 84 tracked in the past 30 days. Many are flagged under third-party jurisdictions like Sierra Leone and Cameroon.

events.title: Japan refiners use ship transfers due to Hormuz disruptions
events.description: Japanese refiners are using VLCCs for ship-to-ship transfers in Malaysia and tapping the US market due to disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz, altering standard crude trade mechanisms.

events.title: Swire Shipping increases rates for Australian and Timor-Leste routes
events.description: Swire Shipping has announced General Rate Increases (GRIs) for cargo shipments to Townsville and Darwin in Australia, as well as Dili in Timor-Leste, effective June 25, 2026, due to rising operating costs and vessel charter rates.

events.title: Hapag-Lloyd updates Africa-bound peak season surcharge
events.location: Asia and Oceania to West Africa and East Africa
events.description: Hapag-Lloyd has updated its Peak Season Surcharge (PSS) for cargo from Asia and Oceania to West Africa and East Africa, effective June 20, 2026. The surcharge adjustment reflects carrier pricing actions during peak shipping periods.

events.title: Kuwait airspace closure disrupts air cargo
events.description: Kuwait airspace was temporarily closed following strikes in the region, causing uncertainty for air cargo operations. DHL has restored its Gulf network, but airlines remain cautious about potential renewed disruptions.

events.title: EU imposes €3 fee on low-value e-commerce imports
events.description: The EU will implement a €3 fee for low-value e-commerce shipments starting 1 July, causing expected volatility in Asia-Europe air cargo markets. Importers are rushing to meet the deadline, with concerns about a surge in pre-deadline volumes and uncertain post-implementation demand patterns.

events.title: US considers revived China ship fees impacting ag exports
events.description: US agriculture exporters warn that revived port fees on Chinese-built ships could add up to $900 per container, threatening export sales of key commodities. Senators Elizabeth Warren and Mark Kelly have pushed for reinstating the fees.

events.title: Hormuz Strait closure impacts Middle East landbridges
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz remains closed following air strikes between Iran and the US, forcing Gulf importers to rely on overland routes and the TIR system. This has increased pressure on landbridges and caused container congestion at Saudi Red Sea ports like Jeddah and King Abdullah.

events.title: U.S. Forces Disable Tanker MT Jalveer in Gulf of Oman
events.description: U.S. forces disabled the MT Jalveer tanker during a blockade enforcement operation in the Gulf of Oman. All 20 Indian crew members were safely evacuated to shore.

events.title: Iran Closes Hormuz Strait to Vessel Traffic
events.description: Following US airstrikes, Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz closed to all vessel traffic, raising concerns over global oil shipments and regional security.

events.title: US container imports decline in April
events.description: Imports through major U.S. container ports fell 5.1% in April compared to March and 7.3% compared to April 2025, according to the Global Port Tracker report.

events.title: EU sanctions drive new tanker sale clause
events.description: BIMCO is drafting a standard clause for tanker sales to comply with EU sanctions targeting Russian involvement in tanker trades, following the 20th sanctions package.

events.title: Three Gorges Dam ship lock expansion begins
events.description: China has started construction on a new five-stage ship lock system at the Three Gorges Dam, aiming to nearly double cargo capacity and reduce congestion on a major inland shipping corridor.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz ceasefire collapses
events.description: A fragile ceasefire in the Strait of Hormuz has collapsed following fresh attacks between the US and Iran. A product tanker, Settebello, suffered a fire off Oman, with three seafarers missing.

events.title: U.S. Policy Shift on Venezuela Energy and Mining Exports
events.description: The U.S. Treasury Department introduced a new sanctions framework to allow U.S. and allied companies to participate in Venezuela's oil, gas, and mineral exports, signaling a significant policy shift that could impact global supply chains.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz disruption raises freight rates
events.description: Freight rates are rising due to ongoing uncertainty around the Strait of Hormuz, with bunker surcharges and expectations of prolonged disruption affecting market sentiment.

events.title: VLCC Disappearances Near Suez
events.description: Ships, particularly VLCCs, are disappearing under the radar, with notable activity reported on both sides of the Suez Canal, indicating potential disruptions in freight flows.

events.title: Ukraine targets Russian logistics in Mariupol
events.description: Ukraine is intensifying its military campaign, targeting Russian logistical operations in Mariupol and other areas, disrupting supply chain movements in the region.

events.title: Cargo ship engaged in gunfire with small boat off Yemen
events.description: A cargo ship exchanged gunfire with a small boat approximately 88 nautical miles from Balhaf, Yemen, in the Gulf of Aden, likely an attempted piracy incident.

events.title: Greek Ferry Damages Port Jetty
events.description: A car ferry collided with a jetty at the entrance to the port of Rafina, Greece, breaking it in half. The ferry sustained no damage, but the incident may affect port operations.

events.title: FedEx pilots ratify new labor agreement
events.description: FedEx pilots have ratified a new labor agreement with ALPA, covering approximately 5,000 pilots. The contract will take effect on June 29 and marks a significant development in the company's airline operations.

events.title: U.S. Operation Assists Ships Through Hormuz Strait
events.description: President Trump claimed the U.S. military conducted a covert operation to assist over 200 commercial ships through the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz, indicating potential geopolitical tensions or disruptions in this key shipping route.

events.title: Truck-to-ship LNG bunkering in Las Palmas
events.description: A dual-fuel ferry was supplied with LNG via truck-to-ship bunkering at the Port of Las Palmas, marking a first for the port and supporting cleaner maritime operations.

events.title: Middle East emergency transit ports activated
events.description: Shipments to Upper Gulf locations are being rerouted and temporarily stored at emergency transit ports due to ongoing disruptions in Middle East trade corridors.

events.title: M/T MARIVEX registration clarification by Palau Ship Registry
events.description: The Palau Ship Registry clarifies that the vessel M/T MARIVEX (IMO 9464156) was not registered under the Palau flag at the time of an incident in the Gulf of Oman, addressing potential confusion in media reports.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Tensions Disrupt Shipping
events.description: Increased tensions between the U.S. and Iran are causing uncertainty about shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global oil transit route.

events.title: Product tanker attacked off Oman, crew missing
events.description: A Palau-flagged product tanker was attacked early on June 10, approximately 20 nautical miles off Sohar, Oman, leaving three crew members missing. The vessel issued a distress call following the incident.

events.title: Suez Canal implements new surcharge regime
events.description: Egypt has introduced a new surcharge regime for the Suez Canal, marking a shift toward ongoing toll extraction for strategic waterways. This affects shipping costs for vessels returning to traditional routes.
